title: SEC0: test_parse_auth_success_response asserts sequence field that relay never sends
description: File: wasm/tests/wasm.rs, lines 52-63. The test hand-crafts a JSON string containing 'sequence':5 in the auth result and then asserts v['result']['sequence'].as_u64() == Some(5). However, AuthenticateResult in core/src/protocol.rs (lines 182-185) has only pub_id and subscription_expires — no sequence field. The relay constructs AuthenticateResult with those two fields only (relay/src/ws.rs line 175-179). The sequence field belongs to UserInfo in DirectoryList params, not the authenticate response. The test asserts a field that does not exist in the actual relay response, creating a false confidence that the auth result contains sequence. If code ever consumed the real relay response and expected a sequence field in auth, it would get None/0 silently. The test must be corrected to reflect the actual AuthenticateResult wire format.
